title: Transport Layer
description: Covers TCP, UDP, ARQ retransmission protocols, and AIMD congestion control.

questions.prompt: Which transport protocol is connection-oriented and provides reliable, in-order delivery?
Which transport protocol is connectionless, has smaller headers, and treats each segment independently?
Match each ARQ protocol to its behaviour.
right: Cumulative ACKs, but individually retransmits problematic packets; uses fast retransmit on 3 duplicate ACKs
Send one packet, wait for its ACK before sending the next
Sender retransmits only the missing packets; receiver buffers out-of-order arrivals
Window-based; if one packet times out, the entire window is retransmitted

questions.prompt: Which of the following are TRUE about AIMD congestion control?
options: Additive increase: window grows by 1 MSS per RTT
Multiplicative decrease: window is halved on loss
After a timeout, TCP enters slow start (window reset and exponential growth until threshold)
After 3 duplicate ACKs, TCP enters slow start
AIMD converges towards a fair share of bandwidth between competing flows

questions.prompt: How many duplicate ACKs does TCP wait for before doing a fast retransmit?
Which of the following does TCP provide (but UDP does not)?
options: Reliable delivery via ACKs and retransmission

questions.prompt: Which of the following are part of the TCP three-way handshake?
options: Client sends ACK (possibly with request) to server

questions.prompt: In TCP, what is the window size computed as (in terms of bytes sent and acknowledged)?
accepted: seq. num of last byte sent minus seq. num of last byte acked
last byte sent minus last byte acked
